subject: [RFC PATCH 12/40] mm: page_alloc: steer movable allocations to fullest clean superpageblocks

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch steers movable allocations in the Linux kernel to use the fullest clean superpageblock, which helps preserve empty superpageblocks for potential huge page allocation. It introduces two new functions: `sb_preferred_for_movable` and `__rmqueue_from_sb`, which are used to find the fullest clean superpageblock and allocate pages from it. The patch also fixes an oversight in the bulk allocator's handling of ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT, ensuring that it doesn't taint clean superpageblocks. This change aims to improve memory allocation efficiency and reduce fragmentation.
